rules.name: Exposure score range: 0-10
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score < 0 OR exposure_score > 10
rules.description: Karpathy's scoring rubric defines a 0-10 integer scale. Values outside this range indicate a parsing failure in the ingestor. The effective range in the data is 1-10 (no zeros observed), but the rubric permits 0, so the DQ rule allows it.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Min: 1, Max: 10. Cardinality: 10 distinct values (scores 1 through 10; score 0 not present).' Domain context: 'Score 0 absent. DQ rule range should remain 0-10 (rubric allows it), but effective range is 1-10.' Spec: 'Exposure score range: 0 <= exposure_score <= 10 (P0 -- hard block).' Threshold: 0 violations in 342 rows.

rules.name: Exposure score completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score IS NULL
rules.description: exposure_score is a required field -- every occupation must have a score. Null exposure score means the scores.json-to-occupations.csv join failed or the source data is incomplete.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 342).' Spec schema: exposure_score is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations in 342 rows.

rules.name: Rationale completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE rationale IS NULL
rules.description: rationale is a required field -- every occupation must have an LLM-generated explanation of its exposure score. Null rationale means the scores.json parse failed for that entry.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 342). Cardinality: 342 distinct values (all unique rationales).' Spec schema: rationale is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations in 342 rows.

rules.name: Slug uniqueness (grain check)
rules.sql: SELECT slug, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure GROUP BY slug HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per occupation slug. Duplicate slugs indicate an ingestor dedup failure or a scores.json parsing error that created duplicate entries.
rules.evidence: EDA: '342 distinct slugs, 342 rows -- grain uniqueness confirmed. Zero duplicate slugs or occupation titles.' Spec grain: [slug]. Threshold: 0 duplicates in 342 rows.

rules.name: Row count within expected range: 325-359
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 325 OR cnt > 359 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure)
rules.description: Karpathy scored exactly 342 BLS occupations. The expected row count is 342 +/- 5% (325-359). A count outside this range suggests a download failure, JSON parse error, or source data change.
rules.evidence: EDA: '342 rows ingested, exactly matching the expected count.' Spec: 'Row count: 342 +/- 5% (P0).' Threshold: 325 <= count <= 359.

rules.name: SOC code format validation (where present)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NOT NULL AND NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Where SOC code is present (non-null), it must match the XX-XXXX format (two digits, hyphen, four digits). soc_code is nullable -- 52 of 342 occupations lack SOC codes, which is expected. But when present, format must be correct.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'All 290 non-null values pass XX-XXXX format validation. Zero malformed codes.' Domain context: 'SOC 2018 codes (XX-XXXX format) -- present for 290 of 342 rows.' Threshold: 0 violations among non-null SOC codes.

rules.name: SOC code coverage: >= 80% non-null
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(null_count AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) > 0.20 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN soc_code IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS null_count FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure)
rules.description: At least 80% of rows must have a non-null soc_code. The spec originally estimated 95% coverage, but EDA revealed actual coverage is 84.8% (290/342). Threshold adjusted to 80% to allow margin below the observed rate. Null SOC codes are concentrated in transportation, installation/repair, production, and computer/IT categories.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'SOC coverage is 84.8% (290/342), not the 95% estimated in the spec. 52 occupations have null SOC codes.' EDA recommendation: 'Set SOC coverage threshold to >= 84% (not 95% as spec estimated). P1 warn.' Threshold adjusted to >= 80% (allowing margin below the 84.8% observed rate).

rules.name: Occupation title completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE occupation_title IS NULL
rules.description: occupation_title is a required field. Every occupation must have a human-readable title from occupations.csv. Null title means the CSV parse or slug-to-row join failed.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 342). Cardinality: 342 distinct values (100% unique).' Spec schema: occupation_title is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations in 342 rows.

rules.name: Cross-validation: median_pay vs BLS OOH (> 20% diff)
rules.sql: SELECT k.slug, k.soc_code, k.median_pay_annual AS karpathy_pay, b.median_annual_wage AS bls_pay, ABS(k.median_pay_annual - b.median_annual_wage) / b.median_annual_wage AS pct_diff FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure k INNER JOIN bronze.bls_ooh b ON k.soc_code = b.soc_code WHERE k.median_pay_annual IS NOT NULL AND b.median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL AND b.median_annual_wage > 0 AND ABS(k.median_pay_annual - b.median_annual_wage) / b.median_annual_wage > 0.20
rules.description: For occupations with matching SOC codes in both Karpathy and BLS OOH, median_pay_annual should agree within 20%. A discrepancy greater than 20% would indicate a data vintage mismatch between Karpathy's BLS snapshot and our pipeline's BLS ingest.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Wage cross-validation shows perfect alignment. For all 241 matched rows with wage data, Karpathy median_pay_annual equals our BLS OOH median_annual_wage exactly ($0 difference on every row). This confirms Karpathy used the same BLS data snapshot as our pipeline.' Domain context: 'Wage alignment with BLS OOH: perfect. Zero discrepancies across 241 comparable rows.' Threshold: 0 violations (will pass trivially given perfect current alignment).

rules.name: Slug completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE slug IS NULL
rules.description: slug is the grain field and join key between scores.json and occupations.csv. It must be present in every row. A null slug means the ingestor produced a row without a valid identifier.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 342). This is the grain field.' Spec schema: slug is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations in 342 rows.

rules.name: Category completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE category IS NULL
rules.description: category is a required field. Every occupation must be assigned to one of Karpathy's 25 BLS category groups.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 342). Cardinality: 25 distinct values.' Spec schema: category is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations in 342 rows.

rules.name: Source URL completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE source_url IS NULL
rules.description: source_url is a required provenance field. Must be populated for every row to establish data lineage.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0%. Cardinality: 1 distinct value (all rows: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/karpathy/jobs/master/scores.json).' Spec schema: source_url is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Ingestion timestamp completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: ingested_at is a required provenance timestamp. Must be populated for every row.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0%. Cardinality: 1 distinct value (single batch ingest).' Spec schema: ingested_at is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Load date completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE load_date IS NULL
rules.description: load_date is a required field. Must be populated for every row.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0%. Cardinality: 1 distinct value.' Spec schema: load_date is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Source method completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE source_method IS NULL
rules.description: source_method is a required provenance field. Must be 'github_download' or 'local_cache'.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0%. Cardinality: 1 distinct value (all rows: github_download).' Spec schema: source_method is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Source method validity: must be github_download or local_cache
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE source_method NOT IN ('github_download', 'local_cache')
rules.description: source_method must be one of the two defined ingestor modes. Any other value indicates an ingestor configuration error.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'All rows: github_download.' Spec defines two valid values: 'github_download' or 'local_cache'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Load date within last 30 days (freshness)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE load_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30' DAY LIMIT 1
rules.description: The load_date should be recent (within 30 days of query execution). Although this is a static snapshot dataset with event-driven refresh, stale data past 30 days during initial pipeline build indicates the table has not been refreshed after the initial load.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'All rows: 2026-04-09.' Consistent with existing freshness rules (RAW-OOH-017). Domain context: 'Static snapshot. Event-driven refresh.' Threshold: 0 violations within 30-day window.

rules.name: Exposure score is integer (no fractional values)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score != CAST(exposure_score AS INT)
rules.description: Karpathy's rubric produces integer scores. If the ingestor parsed exposure_score as a float, fractional values would indicate a type-coercion failure.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Type: INTEGER. Cardinality: 10 distinct values (scores 1 through 10).' Spec schema: exposure_score is int. Threshold: 0 violations.
